revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''大野氏'''（おおのし）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]。[[豊後国]]における有力な在地武士の一族、[[豊後大神氏]]の支流の一...'
revision.text:
'''大野氏'''（おおのし）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]。[[豊後国]]における有力な在地武士の一族、[[豊後大神氏]]の支流の一つで、豊後国[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]大野郷（現在の[[大分県]][[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]][[大野町 (大分県)|大野町]]、[[朝地町]]あたり）を本拠とした。通字は「'''基'''」（もと）。
== 概要 == ''詳しくは「[[豊後大神氏]]」の項を参照のこと。''
本流の豊後大神氏は、大和大神氏の分流である[[大神良臣]]が[[仁和]]2年（[[886年]]）に豊後介を任じられたが、その善政を慕った領民の願いによって、任期後にその子[[大神庶幾|庶幾]]（これちか）が[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]領としてとどめられ、子（曾孫とも）である[[大神惟基|惟基]]がその始祖となったとされている。そして、惟基の子のうち[[大野栄基|栄基]]が領地の名称である「'''大野'''」を苗字としたのが豊後国の大野氏の始まりである。以降の当主も引き続き「基」の字を通字として名前に用いた。
豊後大神氏は、[[大友氏]]が入国する以前から豊後国における有力な在地武士の一族であったが、15代[[大野貞基|貞基]]には嗣子がなく、[[大野著基|著基]]（出自不詳）がその養子という形で跡を継ぐが、この著基以降、大友氏歴代当主（[[大友義鎮|大友義鎮（宗麟）]]を除く）から[[偏諱]]を賜っていることから、これ以降の当主が大友氏に仕えていたことが分かる。
== 歴代当主 == （＊大友氏に仕え始めた16代著基以降、偏諱を与えた主君（当主）の人名を < の右側に示してある。 ） *[[大野栄基]]（よしもと） - [[大神惟基]]の子。 *[[大野盛基]]（もりもと） *[[大野家基]]（いえもと） *[[大野泰基]]（やすもと） *[[大野能基]]（よしもと） *[[大野基定]]（もとさだ） *[[大野秀基]]（ひでもと） *[[大野保基]]（やすもと） *[[大野時基]]（ときもと） *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第10代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） *[[大野宗基]]（むねもと） *[[大野賢基]]（かたもと） *[[大野勅基]]（のりもと） *[[大野基経]]（もとつね） *[[大野貞基]]（さだもと） *[[大野著基]]（あきもと） - 貞基の養子。 < [[大友親著]] *[[大野直基]]（なおもと） < [[大友持直]] *[[大野綱基]]（つなもと） < [[大友親綱]] *[[大野隆基]]（たかもと） < [[大友親隆]] *[[大野繁基]]（しげもと） < [[大友親繁]] *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第21代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） < [[大友政親]] *[[大野右基]]（すけもと） < [[大友義右]] *[[大野治基]]（はるもと） < [[大友親治]] *[[大野長基]]（ながもと） < [[大友義長]] *[[大野鑑基]]（あきもと） < [[大友義鑑]] *[[大野統基]]（むねもと） < [[大友義統]] *[[大野延基]]（のぶもと） *[[大野公基]]（きんもと）
== 主な支流 == *[[朽網氏]] *[[敷戸氏]] *[[大牟田氏]]
''※詳しい系図は「日本の苗字七千傑 大野氏系図」（以下外部リンクより）を参照。''
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm 日本の苗字七千傑 大野氏系図] * [http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F コトバンク 大野氏]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおかし}} [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:豊後国]] [[Category:豊後大神氏|*]] [[Category:大分県の歴史]]

'''大野氏'''（おおのし）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]。[[豊後国]]における有力な在地武士の一族、[[豊後大神氏]]の支流の一つで、豊後国[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]大野郷（現在の[[大分県]][[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]][[大野町 (大分県)|大野町]]、[[朝地町]]あたり）を本拠とした。通字は「'''基'''」（もと）。
== 概要 == ''詳しくは「[[豊後大神氏]]」の項を参照のこと。''
本流の豊後大神氏は、大和大神氏の分流である[[大神良臣]]が[[仁和]]2年（[[886年]]）に豊後介を任じられたが、その善政を慕った領民の願いによって、任期後にその子[[大神庶幾|庶幾]]（これちか）が[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]領としてとどめられ、子（曾孫とも）である[[大神惟基|惟基]]がその始祖となったとされている。そして、惟基の子のうち[[大野栄基|栄基]]が領地の名称である「'''大野'''」を苗字としたのが豊後国の大野氏の始まりである。以降の当主も引き続き「基」の字を通字として名前に用いた。
豊後大神氏は、[[大友氏]]が入国する以前から豊後国における有力な在地武士の一族であったが、15代[[大野貞基|貞基]]には嗣子がなく、[[大野著基|著基]]（出自不詳）がその養子という形で跡を継ぐが、この著基以降、大友氏歴代当主（[[大友義鎮|大友義鎮（宗麟）]]を除く）から[[偏諱]]を賜っていることから、これ以降の当主が大友氏に仕えていたことが分かる。
== 歴代当主 == （＊大友氏に仕え始めた16代著基以降、偏諱を与えた主君（当主）の人名を < の右側に示してある。 ） *[[大野栄基]]（よしもと） - [[大神惟基]]の子。 *[[大野盛基]]（もりもと） *[[大野家基]]（いえもと） *[[大野泰基]]（やすもと） *[[大野能基]]（よしもと） *[[大野基定]]（もとさだ） *[[大野秀基]]（ひでもと） *[[大野保基]]（やすもと） *[[大野時基]]（ときもと） *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第10代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） *[[大野宗基]]（むねもと） *[[大野賢基]]（かたもと） *[[大野勅基]]（のりもと） *[[大野基経]]（もとつね） *[[大野貞基]]（さだもと） *[[大野著基]]（あきもと） - 貞基の養子。 < [[大友親著]] *[[大野直基]]（なおもと） < [[大友持直]] *[[大野綱基]]（つなもと） < [[大友親綱]] *[[大野隆基]]（たかもと） < [[大友親隆]] *[[大野繁基]]（しげもと） < [[大友親繁]] *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第21代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） < [[大友政親]] *[[大野右基]]（すけもと） < [[大友義右]] *[[大野治基]]（はるもと） < [[大友親治]] *[[大野長基]]（ながもと） < [[大友義長]] *[[大野鑑基]]（あきもと） < [[大友義鑑]] *[[大野統基]]（むねもと） < [[大友義統]] *[[大野延基]]（のぶもと） < [[大友義乗|大友義延]] *[[大野公基]]（きんもと）
== 主な支流 == *[[朽網氏]] *[[敷戸氏]] *[[大牟田氏]]
''※詳しい系図は「日本の苗字七千傑 大野氏系図」（以下外部リンクより）を参照。''
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm 日本の苗字七千傑 大野氏系図] * [http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F コトバンク 大野氏]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおかし}} [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:豊後国]] [[Category:豊後大神氏|*]] [[Category:大分県の歴史]]

'''大野氏'''（おおのし）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]。[[豊後国]]における有力な在地武士の一族、[[大神氏 (豊後国)|豊後大神氏]]の支流の一つで、豊後国[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]大野郷（現在の[[大分県]][[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]][[大野町 (大分県)|大野町]]、[[朝地町]]あたり）を本拠とした。通字は「'''基'''」（もと）。
== 概要 == ''詳しくは「[[大神氏 (豊後国)|豊後大神氏]]」の項を参照のこと。''
本流の豊後大神氏は、大和大神氏の分流である[[大神良臣]]が[[仁和]]2年（[[886年]]）に豊後介を任じられたが、その善政を慕った領民の願いによって、任期後にその子[[大神庶幾|庶幾]]（これちか）が[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]領としてとどめられ、子（曾孫とも）である[[大神惟基|惟基]]がその始祖となったとされている。そして、惟基の子のうち[[大野栄基|栄基]]が領地の名称である「'''大野'''」を苗字としたのが豊後国の大野氏の始まりである。以降の当主も引き続き「基」の字を通字として名前に用いた。
豊後大神氏は、[[大友氏]]が入国する以前から豊後国における有力な在地武士の一族であったが、15代[[大野貞基|貞基]]の養子となって跡を継いだ[[大野著基|著基]]（出自不詳）以降の当主が大友氏歴代当主（[[大友義鎮|大友義鎮（宗麟）]]を除く）から[[偏諱]]を賜っていることから、途中から大友氏に仕えていたことがわかる。
== 歴代当主 == （＊大友氏に仕え始めた16代著基以降、偏諱を与えた主君（当主）の人名を < の右側に示してある。 ） *[[大野栄基]]（よしもと） - [[大神惟基]]の子。名は'''政基'''（まさもと）とも。 *[[大野盛基]]（もりもと） *[[大野家基]]（いえもと） *[[大野泰基]]（やすもと） *[[大野能基]]（よしもと） *[[大野基定]]（もとさだ） *[[大野秀基]]（ひでもと） *[[大野保基]]（やすもと） *[[大野時基]]（ときもと） *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第10代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） *[[大野宗基]]（むねもと） *[[大野賢基]]（かたもと） *[[大野勅基]]（のりもと） *[[大野基経]]（もとつね） *[[大野貞基]]（さだもと） *[[大野著基]]（あきもと） - 貞基の養子。 < [[大友親著]] *[[大野直基]]（なおもと） < [[大友持直]] *[[大野綱基]]（つなもと） < [[大友親綱]] *[[大野隆基]]（たかもと） < [[大友親隆]] *[[大野繁基]]（しげもと） < [[大友親繁]] *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第21代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） < [[大友政親]] *[[大野右基]]（すけもと） < [[大友義右]] *[[大野治基]]（はるもと） < [[大友親治]] *[[大野長基]]（ながもと） < [[大友義長]] *[[大野鑑基]]（あきもと） < [[大友義鑑]] *[[大野統基]]（むねもと） < [[大友義統]] *[[大野延基]]（のぶもと） < [[大友義乗|大友義延]] *[[大野公基]]（きんもと）
== 主な支流 == *[[朽網氏]] *[[敷戸氏]] *[[大牟田氏]]

'''大野氏'''（おおのし）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]。[[豊後国]]における有力な在地武士の一族、[[大神氏 (豊後国)|豊後大神氏]]の支流の一つで、豊後国[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]大野郷（現在の[[大分県]][[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]][[大野町 (大分県)|大野町]]、[[朝地町]]あたり）を本拠とした。通字は「'''基'''」（もと）。

本流の豊後大神氏は、大和大神氏の分流である[[大神良臣]]が[[仁和]]2年（[[886年]]）に豊後介を任じられたが、その善政を慕った領民の願いによって、任期後にその子[[大神庶幾|庶幾]]（これちか）が[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]領としてとどめられ、子（曾孫とも）である[[大神惟基|惟基]]がその始祖となったとされている。そして、惟基の子のうち[[大野栄基|栄基]]が領地の名称である「'''大野'''」を苗字としたのが豊後国の大野氏の始まりである。以降の当主も引き続き「基」の字を通字として名前に用いた。
豊後大神氏は、[[大友氏]]が入国する以前から豊後国における有力な在地武士の一族であったが、15代[[大野貞基|貞基]]の養子となって跡を継いだ[[大野著基|著基]]（出自不詳）以降の当主が大友氏歴代当主（[[大友義鎮|大友義鎮（宗麟）]]を除く）から[[偏諱]]を賜っていることから、途中から大友氏に仕えていたことがわかる。
== 歴代当主 == （＊大友氏に仕え始めた16代著基以降、偏諱を与えた主君（当主）の人名を < の右側に示してある。 ） *[[大野栄基]]（よしもと） - [[大神惟基]]の子。名は'''政基'''（まさもと）とも。 *[[大野盛基]]（もりもと） *[[大野家基]]（いえもと） *[[大野泰基]]（やすもと） *[[大野能基]]（よしもと） *[[大野基定]]（もとさだ） *[[大野秀基]]（ひでもと） *[[大野保基]]（やすもと） *[[大野時基]]（ときもと） *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第10代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） *[[大野宗基]]（むねもと） *[[大野賢基]]（かたもと） *[[大野勅基]]（のりもと） *[[大野基経]]（もとつね） *[[大野貞基]]（さだもと） *[[大野著基]]（あきもと） - 貞基の養子。 < [[大友親著]] *[[大野直基]]（なおもと） < [[大友持直]] *[[大野綱基]]（つなもと） < [[大友親綱]] *[[大野隆基]]（たかもと） < [[大友親隆]] *[[大野繁基]]（しげもと） < [[大友親繁]] *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第21代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） < [[大友政親]] *[[大野右基]]（すけもと） < [[大友義右]] *[[大野治基]]（はるもと） < [[大友親治]] *[[大野長基]]（ながもと） < [[大友義長]] *[[大野鑑基]]（あきもと） < [[大友義鑑]] *[[大野統基]]（むねもと） < [[大友義統]] *[[大野延基]]（のぶもと） < [[大友義乗|大友義延]] *[[大野公基]]（きんもと）
== 主な支流 == *[[朽網氏]] *[[敷戸氏]]m *[[大牟田氏]] *[[大野鑑太|稗屋大野氏]] ''※詳しい系図は「日本の苗字七千傑 大野氏系図」（以下外部リンクより）を参照。''

'''大野氏'''（おおのし）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]。[[豊後国]]における有力な在地武士の一族、[[大神氏 (豊後国)|豊後大神氏]]の支流の一つで、豊後国[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]大野郷（現在の[[大分県]][[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]][[大野町 (大分県)|大野町]]、[[朝地町]]あたり）を本拠とした。通字は「'''基'''」（もと）。
== 概要 == ''詳しくは「[[大神氏 (豊後国)|豊後大神氏]]」の項を参照のこと。''
本流の豊後大神氏は、大和大神氏の分流である[[大神良臣]]が[[仁和]]2年（[[886年]]）に豊後介を任じられたが、その善政を慕った領民の願いによって、任期後にその子[[大神庶幾|庶幾]]（これちか）が[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]領としてとどめられ、子（曾孫とも）である[[大神惟基|惟基]]がその始祖となったとされている。そして、惟基の子のうち[[大野栄基|栄基]]が領地の名称である「'''大野'''」を苗字としたのが豊後国の大野氏の始まりである。以降の当主も引き続き「基」の字を通字として名前に用いた。
豊後大神氏は、[[大友氏]]が入国する以前から豊後国における有力な在地武士の一族であったが、15代[[大野貞基|貞基]]の養子となって跡を継いだ[[大野著基|著基]]（出自不詳）以降の当主が大友氏歴代当主（[[大友義鎮|大友義鎮（宗麟）]]を除く）から[[偏諱]]を賜っていることから、途中から大友氏に仕えていたことがわかる。
== 歴代当主 == （＊大友氏に仕え始めた16代著基以降、偏諱を与えた主君（当主）の人名を < の右側に示してある。 ） *[[大野栄基]]（よしもと） - [[大神惟基]]の子。名は'''政基'''（まさもと）とも。 *[[大野盛基]]（もりもと） *[[大野家基]]（いえもと） *[[大野泰基]]（やすもと） *[[大野能基]]（よしもと） *[[大野基定]]（もとさだ） *[[大野秀基]]（ひでもと） *[[大野保基]]（やすもと） *[[大野時基]]（ときもと） *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第10代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） *[[大野宗基]]（むねもと） *[[大野賢基]]（かたもと） *[[大野勅基]]（のりもと） *[[大野基経]]（もとつね） *[[大野貞基]]（さだもと） *[[大野著基]]（あきもと） - 貞基の養子。 < [[大友親著]] *[[大野直基]]（なおもと） < [[大友持直]] *[[大野綱基]]（つなもと） < [[大友親綱]] *[[大野隆基]]（たかもと） < [[大友親隆]] *[[大野繁基]]（しげもと） < [[大友親繁]] *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第21代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） < [[大友政親]] *[[大野右基]]（すけもと） < [[大友義右]] *[[大野治基]]（はるもと） < [[大友親治]] *[[大野長基]]（ながもと） < [[大友義長]] *[[大野鑑基]]（あきもと） < [[大友義鑑]] *[[大野統基]]（むねもと） < [[大友義統]] *[[大野延基]]（のぶもと） < [[大友義乗|大友義延]] *[[大野公基]]（きんもと）
== 主な支流 == *[[朽網氏]] *[[敷戸氏]]m *[[大牟田氏]] *[[大野鑑太|稗屋大野氏]] ''※詳しい系図は「日本の苗字七千傑 大野氏系図」（以下外部リンクより）を参照。''

'''大野氏'''（おおのし）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]。[[豊後国]]における有力な在地武士の一族、[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]]の支流の一つで、豊後国[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]大野郷（現在の[[大分県]][[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]][[大野町 (大分県)|大野町]]、[[朝地町]]あたり）を本拠とした。通字は「'''基'''」（もと）。
== 概要 == ''詳しくは「[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]]」の項を参照のこと。''
本流の[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]](おおがし)、の子孫[[大神良臣]]が[[仁和]]2年（[[886年]]）に豊後介を任じられたが、その善政を慕った領民の願いによって、任期後にその子[[大神庶幾|庶幾]]（これちか）が[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]領としてとどめられ、子（曾孫とも）である[[大神惟基|惟基]]がその始祖となったとされている。そして、惟基の子のうち[[大野栄基|栄基]]が領地の名称である「'''大野'''」を苗字としたのが豊後国の大野氏の始まりである。以降の当主も引き続き「基」の字を通字として名前に用いた。
[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]](おおがし)は、[[大友氏]]が入国する以前から豊後国における有力な在地武士の一族であったが、15代[[大野貞基|貞基]]の養子となって跡を継いだ[[大野著基|著基]]（出自不詳）以降の当主が大友氏歴代当主（[[大友義鎮|大友義鎮（宗麟）]]を除く）から[[偏諱]]を賜っていることから、途中から大友氏に仕えていたことがわかる。
== 歴代当主 == （＊大友氏に仕え始めた16代著基以降、偏諱を与えた主君（当主）の人名を < の右側に示してある。 ） *[[大野栄基]]（よしもと） - [[大神惟基]]の子。名は'''政基'''（まさもと）とも。 *[[大野盛基]]（もりもと） *[[大野家基]]（いえもと） *[[大野泰基]]（やすもと） *[[大野能基]]（よしもと） *[[大野基定]]（もとさだ） *[[大野秀基]]（ひでもと） *[[大野保基]]（やすもと） *[[大野時基]]（ときもと） *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第10代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） *[[大野宗基]]（むねもと） *[[大野賢基]]（かたもと） *[[大野勅基]]（のりもと） *[[大野基経]]（もとつね） *[[大野貞基]]（さだもと） *[[大野著基]]（あきもと） - 貞基の養子。 < [[大友親著]] *[[大野直基]]（なおもと） < [[大友持直]] *[[大野綱基]]（つなもと） < [[大友親綱]] *[[大野隆基]]（たかもと） < [[大友親隆]] *[[大野繁基]]（しげもと） < [[大友親繁]] *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第21代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） < [[大友政親]] *[[大野右基]]（すけもと） < [[大友義右]] *[[大野治基]]（はるもと） < [[大友親治]] *[[大野長基]]（ながもと） < [[大友義長]] *[[大野鑑基]]（あきもと） < [[大友義鑑]] *[[大野統基]]（むねもと） < [[大友義統]] *[[大野延基]]（のぶもと） < [[大友義乗|大友義延]] *[[大野公基]]（きんもと）
== 主な支流 == *[[賀来氏]] *[[朽網氏]] *[[敷戸氏]] *[[大牟田氏]] *[[大野鑑太|稗屋大野氏]] ''※詳しい系図は「日本の苗字七千傑 大野氏系図」（以下外部リンクより）を参照。''
== 外部リンク == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071108042915/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm 日本の苗字七千傑 大野氏系図] * [http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F コトバンク 大野氏]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおかし}} [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:九州地方の氏族]] [[Category:豊後大神氏|*]] [[Category:大分県の歴史]]

revision.text:
'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|en|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|en|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|en|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|en|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|en|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|en|賀来氏}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏}}
''For detailed genealogies, see "Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan" in the external links section.''
== External Links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071108042915/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F-1151081 Kotobank - Ōno Clan]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kyushu clans]] [[Category:Bungo Ōgami clan|*]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[ja:大野氏 (豊後国)]]

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|en|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|en|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|en|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|en|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|en|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.

== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|en|賀来氏}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏}}

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|en|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|en|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|en|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|en|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|en|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|en|賀来氏}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏}}
''For detailed genealogies, see "Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan" in the external links section.''
== External Links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071108042915/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F-1151081 Kotobank - Ōno Clan]

revision.text:
'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏}}
''For detailed genealogies, see "Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan" in the external links section.''
== External Links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071108042915/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F-1151081 Kotobank - Ōno Clan]

revision.text:
'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏}}
''For detailed genealogies, see "Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan" in the external links section.''

Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏}}

revision.text:
'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏}}
''For detailed genealogies, see "Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan" in the external links section.''
== External Links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071108042915/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F-1151081 Kotobank - Ōno Clan]

== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. '''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏}}

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. '''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏}}

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. '''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. '''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏}}

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)

'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|12=draft}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=draft}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町|12=draft}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=draft}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏|12=draft}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏|12=draft}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱|12=draft}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏|12=draft}}
''For detailed genealogies, see "Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan" in the external links section.''
== External Links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071108042915/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F-1151081 Kotobank - Ōno Clan]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
'''大野氏'''（おおのし）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]。[[豊後国]]における有力な在地武士の一族、[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]]の支流の一つで、豊後国[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]大野郷（現在の[[大分県]][[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]][[大野町 (大分県)|大野町]]、[[朝地町]]あたり）を本拠とした。通字は「'''基'''」（もと）。
== 概要 == ''詳しくは「[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]]」の項を参照のこと。''
本流の[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]](おおがし)、の子孫[[大神良臣]]が[[仁和]]2年（[[886年]]）に豊後介を任じられたが、その善政を慕った領民の願いによって、任期後にその子[[大神庶幾|庶幾]]（これちか）が[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]領としてとどめられ、子（曾孫とも）である[[大神惟基|惟基]]がその始祖となったとされている。そして、惟基の子のうち[[大野栄基|栄基]]が領地の名称である「'''大野'''」を苗字としたのが豊後国の大野氏の始まりである。以降の当主も引き続き「基」の字を通字として名前に用いた。
[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]](おおがし)は、[[大友氏]]が入国する以前から豊後国における有力な在地武士の一族であったが、15代[[大野貞基|貞基]]の養子となって跡を継いだ[[大野著基|著基]]（出自不詳）以降の当主が大友氏歴代当主（[[大友義鎮|大友義鎮（宗麟）]]を除く）から[[偏諱]]を賜っていることから、途中から大友氏に仕えていたことがわかる。
== 歴代当主 == （＊大友氏に仕え始めた16代著基以降、偏諱を与えた主君（当主）の人名を < の右側に示してある。 ） *[[大野栄基]]（よしもと） - [[大神惟基]]の子。名は'''政基'''（まさもと）とも。 *[[大野盛基]]（もりもと） *[[大野家基]]（いえもと） *[[大野泰基]]（やすもと） *[[大野能基]]（よしもと） *[[大野基定]]（もとさだ） *[[大野秀基]]（ひでもと） *[[大野保基]]（やすもと） *[[大野時基]]（ときもと） *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第10代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） *[[大野宗基]]（むねもと） *[[大野賢基]]（かたもと） *[[大野勅基]]（のりもと） *[[大野基経]]（もとつね） *[[大野貞基]]（さだもと） *[[大野著基]]（あきもと） - 貞基の養子。 < [[大友親著]] *[[大野直基]]（なおもと） < [[大友持直]] *[[大野綱基]]（つなもと） < [[大友親綱]] *[[大野隆基]]（たかもと） < [[大友親隆]] *[[大野繁基]]（しげもと） < [[大友親繁]] *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第21代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） < [[大友政親]] *[[大野右基]]（すけもと） < [[大友義右]] *[[大野治基]]（はるもと） < [[大友親治]] *[[大野長基]]（ながもと） < [[大友義長]] *[[大野鑑基]]（あきもと） < [[大友義鑑]] *[[大野統基]]（むねもと） < [[大友義統]] *[[大野延基]]（のぶもと） < [[大友義乗|大友義延]] *[[大野公基]]（きんもと）
== 主な支流 == *[[賀来氏]] *[[朽網氏]] *[[敷戸氏]] *[[大牟田氏]] *[[大野鑑太|稗屋大野氏]] ''※詳しい系図は「日本の苗字七千傑 大野氏系図」（以下外部リンクより）を参照。''
== 外部リンク == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071108042915/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm 日本の苗字七千傑 大野氏系図] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F-1151081 コトバンク 大野氏]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおかし}} [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:九州地方の氏族]] [[Category:豊後大神氏|*]] [[Category:大分県の歴史]]

revision.text:
'''大野氏'''（おおのし）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]。[[豊後国]]における有力な在地武士の一族、[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]]の支流の一つで、豊後国[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]大野郷（現在の[[大分県]][[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]][[大野町 (大分県)|大野町]]、[[朝地町]]あたり）を本拠とした。通字は「'''基'''」（もと）。
== 概要 == ''詳しくは「[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]]」の項を参照のこと。''
本流の[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]](おおがし)、の子孫[[大神良臣]]が[[仁和]]2年（[[886年]]）に豊後介を任じられたが、その善政を慕った領民の願いによって、任期後にその子[[大神庶幾|庶幾]]（これちか）が[[大野郡 (大分県)|大野郡]]領としてとどめられ、子（曾孫とも）である[[大神惟基|惟基]]がその始祖となったとされている。そして、惟基の子のうち[[大野栄基|栄基]]が領地の名称である「'''大野'''」を苗字としたのが豊後国の大野氏の始まりである。以降の当主も引き続き「基」の字を通字として名前に用いた。
[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]](おおがし)は、[[大友氏]]が入国する以前から豊後国における有力な在地武士の一族であったが、15代[[大野貞基|貞基]]の養子となって跡を継いだ[[大野著基|著基]]（出自不詳）以降の当主が大友氏歴代当主（[[大友義鎮|大友義鎮（宗麟）]]を除く）から[[偏諱]]を賜っていることから、途中から大友氏に仕えていたことがわかる。
== 歴代当主 == （＊大友氏に仕え始めた16代著基以降、偏諱を与えた主君（当主）の人名を < の右側に示してある。 ） *[[大野栄基]]（よしもと） - [[大神惟基]]の子。名は'''政基'''（まさもと）とも。 *[[大野盛基]]（もりもと） *[[大野家基]]（いえもと） *[[大野泰基]]（やすもと） *[[大野能基]]（よしもと） *[[大野基定]]（もとさだ） *[[大野秀基]]（ひでもと） *[[大野保基]]（やすもと） *[[大野時基]]（ときもと） *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第10代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） *[[大野宗基]]（むねもと） *[[大野賢基]]（かたもと） *[[大野勅基]]（のりもと） *[[大野基経]]（もとつね） *[[大野貞基]]（さだもと） *[[大野著基]]（あきもと） - 貞基の養子。 < [[大友親著]] *[[大野直基]]（なおもと） < [[大友持直]] *[[大野綱基]]（つなもと） < [[大友親綱]] *[[大野隆基]]（たかもと） < [[大友親隆]] *[[大野繁基]]（しげもと） < [[大友親繁]] *[[大野親基 (豊後大野氏第21代当主)|大野親基]]（ちかもと） < [[大友政親]] *[[大野右基]]（すけもと） < [[大友義右]] *[[大野治基]]（はるもと） < [[大友親治]] *[[大野長基]]（ながもと） < [[大友義長]] *[[大野鑑基]]（あきもと） < [[大友義鑑]] *[[大野統基]]（むねもと） < [[大友義統]] *[[大野延基]]（のぶもと） < [[大友義乗|大友義延]] *[[大野公基]]（きんもと）
== 主な支流 == *[[賀来氏]] *[[朽網氏]] *[[敷戸氏]] *[[大牟田氏]] *[[大野鑑太|稗屋大野氏]] ''※詳しい系図は「日本の苗字七千傑 大野氏系図」（以下外部リンクより）を参照。''
== 外部リンク == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071108042915/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm 日本の苗字七千傑 大野氏系図] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F-1151081 コトバンク 大野氏]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおかし}} [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:九州地方の氏族]] [[Category:豊後大神氏|*]] [[Category:大分県の歴史]]

Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)]]
revision.text:
'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province|qq=}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno Town (Ōita)|ja|大野町 (大分県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Town (Ōita)|qq=}} and {{ill|Asaji Town|ja|朝地町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asaji Town|qq=}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Clan|qq=}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese courtesy names|qq=}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku Clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuchiumi Clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikito Clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuta Clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hieya Ōno Clan|qq=}}
''For detailed genealogies, see "Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan" in the external links section.''
== External Links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071108042915/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F-1151081 Kotobank - Ōno Clan]

The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. '''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day [[:en:Ōno, Ōita|Ōno Town (Ōita)]] and [[:en:Asaji, Ōita|Asaji Town]], Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the [[:en:Miwa clan|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)]], which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese courtesy names|qq=}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. revision.comment: Merge [[Category:九州地方の氏族]] into [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day [[:en:Ōno, Ōita|Ōno Town (Ōita)]] and [[:en:Asaji, Ōita|Asaji Town]], Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the [[:en:Miwa clan|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)]], which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese courtesy names|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku Clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuchiumi Clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikito Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuta Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hieya Ōno Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
''For detailed genealogies, see "Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan" in the external links section.''

<!--Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)--><!--Ōno Clan (Bungo Province)--> [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:Ōgami Clan of Bungo Province]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11439666]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day [[:en:Ōno, Ōita|Ōno Town (Ōita)]] and [[:en:Asaji, Ōita|Asaji Town]], Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the [[:en:Miwa clan|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)]], which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese courtesy names|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11439666]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day [[:en:Ōno, Ōita|Ōno Town (Ōita)]] and [[:en:Asaji, Ōita|Asaji Town]], Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|qq=|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the [[:en:Miwa clan|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)]], which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese courtesy names|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64026473}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏 (豊後国)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11439666]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day [[:en:Ōno, Ōita|Ōno Town (Ōita)]] and [[:en:Asaji, Ōita|Asaji Town]], Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the [[:en:Miwa clan|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)]], which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the [[:en:Miwa clan|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)]], which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64026473}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
