<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏 (豊後国)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏 (豊後国)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏 (豊後国)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11439666]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno, Ōita|en|Ōno, Ōita|WD=Q8081655|lt=Ōno Town (Ōita)}} and {{ill|Asaji, Ōita|en|Asaji, Ōita|WD=Q4803409|lt=Asaji Town}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Miwa clan|en|Miwa clan|WD=Q11438330|lt=Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo Clan}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64026473}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku Clan|1=Kaku Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682559}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuchiumi Clan|1=Kutami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521650}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikito Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuta Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hieya Ōno Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
''For detailed genealogies, see "Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan" in the external links section.''
== External Links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071108042915/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F-1151081 Kotobank - Ōno Clan]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏 (豊後国)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏 (豊後国)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏 (豊後国)]]
'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno, Ōita|en|Ōno, Ōita|WD=Q8081655|lt=Ōno Town (Ōita)|1=Ōno, Ōita}} and {{ill|Asaji, Ōita|en|Asaji, Ōita|WD=Q4803409|lt=Asaji Town|1=Asaji, Ōita}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Miwa clan|en|Miwa clan|WD=Q11438330|lt=Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Miwa clan}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo Clan|1=Ōtomo clan}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=Taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64026473}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku Clan|1=Q28682559|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682559}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuchiumi Clan|1=Kutami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521650}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikito Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuta Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hieya Ōno Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
''For detailed genealogies, see "Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan" in the external links section.''
== External Links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071108042915/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02106b.htm Japanese Surnames: Seven Thousand Nobles - Ōno Clan] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E9%87%8E%E6%B0%8F-1151081 Kotobank - Ōno Clan]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏 (豊後国)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏 (豊後国)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大野氏 (豊後国)]]
'''Ōno Clan''' (大野氏) was a Japanese clan based in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}, descending from the prominent {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}. The Ōno clan held significant local power in Bungo Province, with their base in the Ōno District (present-day {{ill|Ōno, Ōita|en|Ōno, Ōita|WD=Q8081655|lt=Ōno Town (Ōita)|1=Ōno, Ōita}} and {{ill|Asaji, Ōita|en|Asaji, Ōita|WD=Q4803409|lt=Asaji Town|1=Asaji, Ōita}}, Ōita Prefecture). The clan's hereditary name marker (通字) was "'''Moto'''" (基).
== Overview == ''See also: {{ill|Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|ja|大神氏 (豊後国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Ōga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438332}}''
The Ōno clan originated as a branch of the {{ill|Miwa clan|en|Miwa clan|WD=Q11438330|lt=Ōgami Clan (Bungo Province)|1=Miwa clan}}, which was among the influential samurai families in Bungo Province before the arrival of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo Clan|1=Ōtomo clan}}. According to tradition, Ōgami Yoshiomi, a descendant of the Ōgami family, was appointed as the Deputy Governor of Bungo Province in 886 (Ninna 2). Following his tenure, the local populace petitioned for him to remain as a regional administrator. His son (or great-grandson) Ōgami Korechika was entrusted with the Ōno District, and his descendant Ōgami Koremoto became the progenitor of the Ōno clan. Koremoto's son, Ōno Yoshimoto (栄基), adopted the name "Ōno" from the district, marking the beginning of the Ōno clan in Bungo.
The Ōno clan maintained the hereditary use of "Moto" (基) in the names of its successive heads. By the 16th generation, the clan entered the service of the Ōtomo family. Subsequent clan heads received {{ill|Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja|偏諱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese courtesy names|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=Taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64026473}} from successive Ōtomo lords (except for Ōtomo Sōrin), signifying their loyalty and vassalage.
== Successive Clan Heads == (* Indicates service under the Ōtomo clan. Courtesy name granted by the Ōtomo lord is listed to the right.) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野栄基) - Founder. Name also recorded as Masamoto (政基). * Ōno Morimoto (大野盛基) * Ōno Iemoto (大野家基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野泰基) * Ōno Yoshimoto (大野能基) * Ōno Motosada (大野基定) * Ōno Hidemoto (大野秀基) * Ōno Yasumoto (大野保基) * Ōno Tokimoto (大野時基) * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) * Ōno Munemoto (大野宗基) * Ōno Katamoto (大野賢基) * Ōno Norimoto (大野勅基) * Ōno Mototsune (大野基経) * Ōno Sadamoto (大野貞基) * Ōno Akimoto (大野著基) - Adopted son of Sadamoto. < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Naomoto (大野直基) < Ōtomo Mochinao * Ōno Tsunamoto (大野綱基) < Ōtomo Chikatsuna * Ōno Takamoto (大野隆基) < Ōtomo Chikataka * Ōno Shigemoto (大野繁基) < Ōtomo Chikashige * Ōno Chikamoto (大野親基) < Ōtomo Masachika * Ōno Sukemoto (大野右基) < Ōtomo Yusuke * Ōno Harumoto (大野治基) < Ōtomo Chikaharu * Ōno Nagamoto (大野長基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Akimoto (大野鑑基) < Ōtomo Yoshinaga * Ōno Munemoto (大野統基) < Ōtomo Yoshimune * Ōno Nobumoto (大野延基) < Ōtomo Yoriaki * Ōno Kinmoto (大野公基)
== Notable Branch Families == * {{ill|Kaku Clan|ja|賀来氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku Clan|1=Q28682559|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682559}} * {{ill|Kuchiumi Clan|ja|朽網氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuchiumi Clan|1=Kutami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521650}} * {{ill|Shikito Clan|ja|敷戸氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikito Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōmuta Clan|ja|大牟田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuta Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hieya Ōno Clan|ja|稗屋大野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hieya Ōno Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
